instructions: Each item below is a MEDIUM/LOW/CONFLICT finding that was NOT auto-applied. Review each recommendation and apply manually if you agree. Items are ordered by priority.

supporting_sources.name: Autowin citing Adrian Streather 'Porsche 996 The Essential Companion' (Porsche Archive data)
supporting_sources.note: Streather's book draws from official Porsche factory production records

conflicting_source.name: Supercar Nostalgia (source used for tonight's manual additions)

items.recommendation: APPLY — three sources including Porsche archive-based data agree on 17,298. The 15-unit discrepancy from Supercar Nostalgia is likely a transcription error in that source.
items.apply_instruction: Change '17,283 Coupes' to '17,298 Coupes' in the Carrera 4S variant production field.
items.cabriolet_status: 5,757 CONFIRMED correct — all three sources agree, no change needed.

supporting_sources.name: Elferspot
supporting_sources.note: Elferspot gives 5,142 — very close but slightly lower than 5,152

conflicting_source.name: Supercar Nostalgia (source used for tonight's manual additions)

items.recommendation: APPLY 5,152 — Stuttcars and supercars.net both specifically state 5,152; Elferspot's 5,142 is within noise. Supercar Nostalgia (5,089) is the outlier. If applying, use 5,152 not 5,142.
items.apply_instruction: Change '5,089 worldwide' to '5,152 worldwide' in the Targa variant production field.

items.note: Worldwide was just auto-applied (AA-006). This item is for the US figure only.
items.status: Already applied in AA-006 with ~960 from forum data. User should verify.

source.name: 6SpeedOnline forum compilation (Divexxtreme)
source.note: Single source; prior figure ~1,010 was also unverified. The ~960 figure is plausible and consistent with the worldwide ratio.

items.recommendation: Verify — if you can confirm from a second source, the ~960 stands. If not confirmed, revert US figure to ~1,010 until verified.

items.recommended_value: DELETE or REPLACE with accurate figure
items.analysis: The figure ~37,750 does not reconcile with the per-variant data. All 996 Carrera Coupes across both generations = 31,688 (996.1) + 14,629 (996.2) = 46,317. All 996.1 coupes (Carrera + C4) = 31,688 + 12,720 = 44,408. None of these equal ~37,750. This field appears to predate the detailed per-variant research and should be recomputed.

suggested_replacement_options.explanation: Total 996.1 Carrera-family (coupe+cab) from the per-variant data

items.recommendation: Replace with a meaningful and accurate summary. Suggest: '55,562 Carrera (coupe + cab); 22,284 Carrera 4 (coupe + cab)' — this directly matches the per-variant data.

items.current_value: ~29,380 Carrera Coupes worldwide; ~17,290 Carrera 4S Coupes worldwide

analysis.carrera_coupes_claim: ~29,380 Carrera Coupes — does not reconcile with per-variant data. Carrera Coupe (14,629) + Carrera Cab (14,961) = 29,590 (close but not exactly 29,380; may be using the rounded display values from the manual prompt). OR Carrera Coupe (14,629) + C4 Coupe (3,231) + Targa (5,089) + 40 Jahre (1,963) = 24,912. No combination of per-variant figures produces ~29,380 exactly.
analysis.c4s_coupes_claim: ~17,290 Carrera 4S Coupes — should be 17,298 per ST-001 recommendation (or 17,283 per current file). The ~17,290 is a rounding of the existing figure.
analysis.likely_intent: The ~29,380 likely represents Carrera Coupe + Carrera Cabriolet combined (14,629 + 14,961 = 29,590 ≈ 29,380 using the rounded display values ~14,500 + ~15,000 = ~29,500). This is a plausible reading but should be documented as 'Carrera (coupe + cab)' not 'Carrera Coupes'.

items.recommendation: Update to: '29,590 Carrera RWD (coupe + cab); 17,298 Carrera 4S (coupe + cab combined 23,055)' OR reword to clarify what is being counted. The C4S Cabriolet (5,757) should be added for a complete 4S total.

items.analysis: The ~245 US figure for GT2 Mk1 cannot be verified from available sources. Forum data shows TOTAL 996 GT2 US production (Mk1 + Mk2 combined) was 317 units. If Mk2 was ~72 US, then Mk1 ~245 is plausible; if Mk2 was fewer, Mk1 could be higher. The split between Mk1 and Mk2 in the US is not in available sources.
items.recommendation: Either verify the ~245 US figure against a second source, or remove the US figure and note 'worldwide' only. The total GT2 US production (317) could be cited instead.
